question.text: Which of the following intermediaries is SEBI empowered to register and regulate under Section 11(2)?

answer.text: Stock brokers, share transfer agents, and merchant bankers
Portfolio managers, investment advisers, and underwriters
Depositories, custodians, and credit rating agencies

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 11(2) empowers SEBI to register and regulate a comprehensive list of intermediaries including stock brokers, sub-brokers, share transfer agents, bankers to an issue, trustees of trust deeds, registrars, merchant bankers, underwriters, portfolio managers, investment advisers, depositories, custodians, FIIs, CRAs, and others as notified. **The Trap:** All three options list valid SEBI-regulated intermediaries. The complete list under Section 11(2) covers virtually all securities market intermediaries. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SEBI's regulatory ambit extends to ALL intermediaries in the securities market — this comprehensive jurisdiction is the foundation of the unified regulatory framework.
